hero.title: Translate PDF Online
hero.description: Translate PDFs with layout preserved — columns, tables, figures, and fonts stay faithful. Supports English, Chinese, Spanish, French, Italian, Greek, Japanese, Korean, and more. Runs in your browser; no install.
hero.tip: Special launch pricing for early users

introduce.title: What is Translate PDF Online
introduce.label: Product
introduce.description: A web service for translating PDF documents while keeping the original design. Built for papers, manuals, reports, and books — not plain text dumps.

items.title: Layout-preserving output
items.description: Translated PDFs mirror structure: multi-column text, tables, images, and headings stay aligned with the source.

items.title: Many language pairs
items.description: Choose source and target from a growing set of languages. Fine for academic and professional documents.

items.title: Secure, ephemeral storage
items.description: Files are transferred over HTTPS and removed on a fixed retention schedule after processing.

items.title: History & task recovery
items.description: The History panel lists your uploads and jobs so you can reopen a task without re-uploading the same PDF.

benefits.title: Why teams choose us
benefits.label: Benefits
benefits.description: Focus on the document, not on fixing broken formatting after translation.

items.title: Built for dense PDFs
items.description: Tuned for academic and technical layouts — equations, figures, and tables where the pipeline supports them.

items.title: Side-by-side preview
items.description: Compare source and translated pages in the browser with synced scrolling before you download.

items.title: Optional page ranges
items.description: Translate the whole file or only the pages you need to save time and credits.

usage.title: How to translate your PDF
usage.description: Three steps from upload to download:

items.title: Upload
items.description: Drop a PDF or pick a file. Large documents are supported within the limits shown on the translate page.

items.title: Choose languages & start
items.description: Pick source and target, optionally set a page range, then start translation. Track progress on the same screen.

items.title: Download
items.description: When the job finishes, download the translated PDF. Reopen anytime from History while the file is still available.

features.title: What you get
features.description: Practical capabilities for real PDF workflows.

items.title: Vector-friendly pipeline
items.description: Uses a BabelDOC-style path so text is placed back into the PDF structure where supported.

items.title: Credits & billing
items.description: Pay for what you translate with a credits model tied to pages and product settings.

items.title: Account or guest flow
items.description: Sign in for billing and history across devices, or try the flow in a single session.

items.title: Edge-hosted UI
items.description: Fast first load on Cloudflare; your browser handles preview and download.

items.title: Documentation
items.description: Architecture, retention, and compliance notes in /docs for transparency.

items.title: Developer-friendly stack
items.description: Next.js app with clear APIs between UI, storage, and the translation worker.

stats.title: Built for serious documents
stats.description: From single papers to large manuals.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: More detail in our documentation or email hello@translatepdfonline.com.

items.question: What is Translate PDF Online and how does it work?
items.answer: You upload a PDF, choose source and target languages, and we run a layout-aware translation pipeline. You download a new PDF that mirrors the original structure where the technology supports it.

items.question: Do I need to install anything?
items.answer: No. Upload and preview run in the browser. Files are sent over HTTPS.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: The translate page lists available source and target languages (e.g. English, Chinese, Spanish, French, Italian, Greek, Japanese, Korean). We add pairs over time.

items.question: How long does translation take?
items.answer: Short PDFs often finish in minutes. Long or image-heavy files take longer. You can stay on the page or return later and open the task from History.

items.question: Is my document secure?
items.answer: Traffic is encrypted. Stored objects follow the retention policy described in our docs (typically automatic deletion after about seven days unless stated otherwise).

items.question: Can I translate only specific pages?
items.answer: Yes. Set a page range when you start the job to translate part of the document.

items.question: How do I reopen or preview a translation I did earlier?
items.answer: Open the translate page and use History. Pick a past job to see its status and preview the source and translated PDFs in the browser. You do not need to upload the file again while it is still available under our retention policy.

cta.title: Translate your first PDF
cta.description: Upload on the translate page — use History to return to any recent job.

subscribe.title: Subscribe to our newsletter
subscribe.description: Get product updates from Translate PDF Online.
